bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting maximum utility by proposing my top value on every issue. The opponent opened with the lowest-utility value for me on all issues, so there is not yet evidence of concession or a mutually acceptable tradeoff.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while avoiding repeating my exact previous all-valueA offer. The opponent has already moved from all valueD to valueB on my most important issueA, suggesting some willingness to concede, but their remaining values stay at valueD; I will concede only on my lowest-weight issueD for now while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

The opponent’s offers have improved substantially for me, rising from very low utility to about 0.60 by their latest bid, so they appear to be conceding. Since it is still very early at t=0.04, I will keep firm on my highest-weight issues A and B while making a modest concession on lower-weight issues to avoid repeating my previous bid.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.814 because it is still very early at t=0.06, so there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent’s offers have improved substantially for me on issueA and issueB, but they continue to hold issueC and issueD at valueD, so I concede mainly on lower-weight issueD and partially on issueC while holding firm on the more important issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.08), so there is little need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA and issueB toward my preferred values but is still holding issueC and issueD at valueD, so I will keep my high-weight issues fixed and test whether they can move on issueC while already conceding the low-weight issueD.

I targeted a high but not maximal utility bid around 0.841, since it is still early at t=0.10 but the opponent’s offers have clearly improved for me up to issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueD. Their behavior suggests they may accept concessions on issueC while holding issueD low, so I concede issueC to valueC but ask for a small gain on the least important issueD rather than repeating my previous exact bid.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.866 because it is still early in the negotiation, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s current 0.814-equivalent offer yet. The opponent has conceded strongly on issueA and issueB and seems to hold issueD at valueD, which is low weight for me, so I concede issueD while asking for an improvement on issueC.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while showing some flexibility on the lowest-weight issue. The opponent has steadily conceded on issueA and issueB but is holding issueC at valueC and issueD at valueD, so I will keep my strongest positions on the most important issues and concede issueD while asking for issueC at valueB as a reasonable midpoint.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid rather than matching the opponent’s latest offer outright. The opponent has steadily conceded on issueA and issueB but is holding issueC at valueC and issueD at valueD, so I will concede on low-weight issueD while continuing to ask for valueB on issueC to preserve utility.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.866 because it is still early at t=0.18 and there is no need to drop immediately to the opponent’s repeated offer. The opponent has conceded on issueA and issueB but is holding firm on issueC=valueC and issueD=valueD, so I continue conceding the low-weight issueD while holding issueC at valueB for now.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.866 by holding firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while conceding the lowest-weight issueD. The opponent has steadily conceded up to issueA=valueA and issueB=valueA but is now repeatedly holding at issueC=valueC and issueD=valueD; since time is still early at t=0.20, I will continue asking for a modest improvement on issueC rather than accept their current 0.814-utility offer.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.814 by keeping my highest-weight issues A and B at valueA while conceding on lower-weight issueC and leaving issueD at valueD. Although the time is still relatively early, the opponent has repeatedly held at issueA=valueA, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueD since t=0.10, so matching that stable bid is a reasonable concession to secure agreement while preserving strong value for me.
